# Nat20

> Nat20 is an open-source, pure-Python **D&D 5e SRD 5.2 (CC-BY-4.0) rules
> engine** and typed content dataset. It deterministically resolves combat,
> ability/skill/saving-throw checks, ongoing effects, and 2-D grid movement.
> The engine performs zero I/O and is host-agnostic — it returns typed events
> and outcomes, leaving rendering, narration, transport, and persistence to
> the host. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Wizards of the
> Coast; it uses only System Reference Document (SRD 5.2) content.

Two packages in one uv workspace:

- `dnd5e-engine` — the rules engine (combat loop, checks, effects, grid).
- `dnd5e-srd-data` — the CC-BY-4.0 SRD 5.2 content corpus, a typed
  Foundry-aligned dataset loaded via `BundledAssetLoader`.

Core public API (`dnd5e_engine.__all__`):

- Combat loop: `start_combat`, `submit_player_intent`, `advance_monster_turn`,
  `end_combat`, `narration_events`, `get_actor_active_effects`.
- One-shot checks: `resolve_check` (with `CheckSpec` / `CheckResult` /
  `CheckKind`).
- Character building: `make_build_spec`, `build_party_member` (with
  `CharacterBuildSpec`, `CombatInstance`, `AbilityScores`).
- Specs and grid: `PartyMemberSpec`, `EncounterMemberSpec`, `GridScene`,
  `SceneTopology`, `ZoneEdge`, `cell_id`, `parse_cell`.
- Effects: `ActiveEffect`, `ActiveEffectChange`, `ActiveEffectDuration`.
- Results: `StartCombatResult`, `EndCombatResult`, `CombatOutcome`,
  `DeathRecord`, `LootDrop`, `PlayerIntent`, `CombatHandle`, `IntentType`,
  `ActionType`, `CombatEvent`.

## Docs

- [Home / positioning](index.md): what Nat20 is and why.
- [Quickstart](quickstart.md): install + a ~20-line grid combat, a skill
  check, and character building.
- [Combat model](concepts/combat.md): the handle-driven turn loop.
- [Activities](concepts/activities.md): typed activity corpus driving
  resolution.
- [Effects](concepts/effects.md): the `ActiveEffect` document model and its
  combat-scoped lifecycle.
- [Grid](concepts/grid.md): `GridScene`, cell ids, and movement.
- [API reference](api.md): rendered from source docstrings via mkdocstrings, covering the public surface.
- [Comparison](comparison.md): what it is, what it isn't, vs alternatives.

## Keywords

python D&D 5e engine, 5e SRD rules engine, TTRPG combat engine, deterministic
5e combat library, SRD 5.2, grid combat, skill checks, character builder.
